name: Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)
name_original: Entre Vales Fontes e Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — percurso circular (PR2 SBA)
region_intro: Jogging routes around São Brás De Alportel traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive cork oak forests, and the foothills of the Serra do Caldeirão mountains. The region offers diverse terrain, from gentle paths through pine and eucalyptus trees to more challenging routes with varied elevations. This area, known for its "good airs," provides a tranquil environment for running amidst natural features. Best jogging routes around São Brás De Alportel The most popular jogging route is Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA), an 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the varied landscapes of the Serra do Caldeirão. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from São Brás de Alportel, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This route offers a quick and accessible running experience around the town's surroundings. Local runners also love the Alto da Arroteia Viewpoint loop from Igreja do Alportel, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through the countryside to a scenic viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around São Brás De Alportel is defined by cork oak forests, rolling hills, and the foothills of the Serra do Caldeirão. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding trails. The routes in São Brás De Alportel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 230 runners have used komoot to explore São Brás De Alportel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around São Brás de Alportel?
A: The running routes in São Brás de Alportel feature diverse terrain, from gentle paths through pine and eucalyptus trees to more challenging routes with varied elevations. You'll encounter rolling hills, extensive cork oak forests (&lt;em&gt;montados&lt;/em&gt;), and the foothills of the Serra do Caldeirão mountains. The region is known for its 'good airs' and tranquil environment, making it ideal for outdoor exercise.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in São Brás de Alportel?
A: Yes, São Brás de Alportel offers routes suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. For a quick and accessible running experience around the town, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10290711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from São Brás de Alportel&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.1 km path.

Q: Where can I find longer distance running trails near São Brás de Alportel?
A: For longer distance running, you can explore routes that delve deeper into the Serra do Caldeirão. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422466836/between-vales-fontes-and-memorias-da-serra-do-caldeirao-circular-route-pr2-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 18.3 km and explores varied landscapes. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10292086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Igreja do Alportel&lt;/a&gt;, covering 15.3 km.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around São Brás de Alportel are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422466836/between-vales-fontes-and-memorias-da-serra-do-caldeirao-circular-route-pr2-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32180369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Arroteia Viewpoint loop from Igreja do Alportel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in São Brás de Alportel?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll run through extensive cork oak forests, characteristic of the Algarve. The Serra do Caldeirão mountains offer scenic views and varied terrain. You might also encounter viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de São Miguel&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroteia Summit&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic vistas of the surrounding countryside.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in São Brás de Alportel?
A: The running routes in São Brás de Alportel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 230 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the tranquil environment and the beauty of the cork oak forests and rolling hills.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the running trails?
A: Yes, several routes lead to scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32180369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Arroteia Viewpoint loop from Igreja do Alportel&lt;/a&gt; takes you directly to a beautiful vista. Other notable high points offering views include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de São Miguel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4037493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcaria do Cume and the Atlantic Ocean&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in São Brás de Alportel?
A: São Brás de Alportel is known as 'Terra de Bons Ares' (Land of Good Airs) due to its favorable climatic conditions. This makes it a pleasant environment for outdoor exercise throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for running, with lush landscapes and wildflowers. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winters are mild.

Q: Are there any routes that go through the famous cork oak forests?
A: Absolutely. The region is deeply connected to its cork heritage, and many routes traverse the extensive cork oak forests, known locally as 'montados'. These shaded trails offer a unique experience, allowing runners to appreciate both the ecological and cultural value of the area. The 'Rota da Cortiça' is a well-known trail that specifically highlights these forests.

Q: How many running routes are available in São Brás de Alportel?
A: There are over 20 running routes available around São Brás de Alportel, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. This includes 1 easy route, 17 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: Can I find routes that offer a mix of forest and mountain scenery?
A: Yes, the routes in São Brás de Alportel often combine different landscapes. You can find trails that wind through the peaceful cork oak forests and then ascend into the foothills of the Serra do Caldeirão mountains, providing a dynamic running experience with varied scenery and elevations.
tips: Parises is a very peaceful village located along the Via Algarviana. It offers an ideal starting point for hiking trails in the surrounding area. There's also a small café where you can buy something to eat and drink.

Ribeira das Ruivas is a small intermittent watercourse. Your crossing takes place at this point, however it is very common, in times when it doesn't rain, to not find any water here. When there is water, however, be prepared to either do without shoes or to rinse them.
Fronteira is a small group of houses located in the interior of the Algarve. The place provides very beautiful views of the region, although there is not much to see in the village itself.
